description: Seed patterns for the audit/concurrency-race family. Hits target lock primitives, atomic operations, async coordination, transaction scopes, and signal handlers. The skill verifies invariants under concurrent execution: shared mutable state guards, TOCTOU windows, race conditions in identity checks.
notes: Race conditions are the hardest class to find statically. Most real findings require dynamic tooling (ThreadSanitizer, race-aware fuzzing). The skill should focus on visible-from-static patterns: missing locks, unsafe shared state, TOCTOU between check and use.
False positives are common because static cannot prove the absence of a race; the skill must clearly distinguish 'visible TOCTOU window' from 'speculative concern'.

c_cpp.false_positive_notes: Stable. Investigate lock-unlock pairing on every exit path.
Atomic does not imply correct. Check memory ordering.
Async-signal-safe rules apply; very few functions are safe in handlers.

c_cpp.description: volatile usage — often misused as a substitute for atomic
c_cpp.false_positive_notes: volatile does NOT provide atomicity or memory ordering in C/C++.

go.false_positive_notes: Stable; check whether cancellation actually stops work.

javascript_typescript.description: Promise.race/all — pattern for racing async ops
javascript_typescript.false_positive_notes: Common; check cancellation handling on losers.

python.false_positive_notes: Check that cancellation handlers leave state consistent.

python.description: Thread / executor pool start — concurrent task launch. Audit shared state guards.
asyncio task fan-out — gather/wait/TaskGroup. Multiple concurrent coros sharing state.
python.false_positive_notes: gather() with mutating shared dict is race-prone.

python.description: global / nonlocal declarations — shared mutable state across functions. Race-prone in async/threaded code.
python.false_positive_notes: Common in module setup; risk is when global is mutated from concurrent contexts.

python.description: async with / with on lock — explicit critical-section. Positive signal that developer is aware.
multiprocessing primitives — true parallelism across CPUs. Shared state across processes is non-trivial.
python.false_positive_notes: Pickling overhead + race conditions on Manager dict/list are subtle.

python.description: Celery task — distributed worker, MUST be idempotent. Class-level state is shared per worker.

java.false_positive_notes: Provides visibility but not atomicity for compound ops.

java.description: Double-checked locking idiom — broken pre-volatile JDK5
java.false_positive_notes: Common pattern; correct only with volatile holder.
Their use is good; investigate compound check-then-act patterns.
Investigate lambda captures of mutable state.

java.description: Executor framework — thread pool / task submission
Direct Thread instantiation / start — usually a smell vs Executor
Thread.sleep / wait/notify — low-level synchronisation, often bugs
java.false_positive_notes: Investigate; wait should be in a loop.

java.description: Public static mutable collection — racy without sync
java.false_positive_notes: Investigate whether the field is final + immutable wrapper.
Stable; investigate captured state in @Async methods.

csharp.description: C# lock keyword / Monitor.Enter — fundamental sync

csharp.false_positive_notes: Their use is good; investigate compound check-then-act.
async void in non-event handlers = unobserved exception.

csharp.description: Task.Run / ThreadPool — captured state in lambdas

csharp.false_positive_notes: Provides visibility; doesn't atomicise compound ops.

kotlin.description: Kotlin coroutine launch — fire-and-forget; investigate cancellation/scope leakage
kotlin.false_positive_notes: GlobalScope.launch is the dangerous form (no cancellation). Structured concurrency preferred.
Common; investigate lazy vs eager start.

kotlin.description: runBlocking — blocks thread; investigate use outside main/test
kotlin.false_positive_notes: Should NEVER appear in production server code outside main.

kotlin.description: withContext — context switch; investigate cancellation cooperation
Kotlin Flow / Channel — async streams, investigate backpressure + cancellation

php.description: File-system advisory lock (flock) — primary PHP cross-process mutex. Audit non-blocking + release paths.
php.false_positive_notes: LOCK_UN without prior LOCK_EX in scope is a bug. Missing release on exception path is the classic finding.

php.description: Session lock release — PHP's default session backend serialises requests to the SAME user via session.lock. Missing close is a self-DoS source.
php.false_positive_notes: session_write_close is correct usage; FN is when it's missing entirely from long-running endpoints.

php.description: POSIX shared memory + semaphores + message queues — IPC primitives, race-prone without careful pairing
php.false_positive_notes: Each acquire must have a release on every code path including exceptions.

php.description: Symfony Lock component — distributed locks (Redis, Doctrine, semaphore stores)
php.false_positive_notes: Auto-release on script end exists for some stores but not all; explicit release is safer.

php.description: Doctrine ORM lock modes — DB row locking through ORM
php.false_positive_notes: OPTIMISTIC requires a @Version field; absence is a bug indicator.

php.description: MySQL named locks + row locks — explicit DB-level mutual exclusion
php.false_positive_notes: FOR UPDATE outside a transaction is a no-op. Look at the surrounding tx boundary.

php.description: PCNTL multiprocess primitives — fork-based concurrency in CLI workers
php.false_positive_notes: Rare in web stacks; common in long-running CLI workers. Inherited file descriptors / DB connections are the FP-source.

php.description: ext-parallel — true multithreading in modern PHP (requires ZTS build)

swift.description: Swift actor declaration — protects state via mailbox serialization. Audit nonisolated members + reentrancy.
swift.false_positive_notes: Stable Swift 5.5+ marker. Audit: are there nonisolated mutable properties?

swift.description: @unchecked Sendable — manual claim of thread safety, BYPASSES the compiler's data-race check. High-risk if claim is wrong.
swift.false_positive_notes: Almost always a smell. Audit: is the type really thread-safe? If it has mutable properties without locks, NO.

swift.description: @MainActor isolation — guarantees main-thread execution but cross-actor hop is async. @MainActor(unsafe) bypasses isolation checks.
swift.false_positive_notes: @MainActor on UI code is correct. @MainActor(unsafe) is the audit-relevant variant.

swift.description: Task.detached — escapes structured concurrency; no parent context inheritance. Common source of stranded work + lost cancellation.
swift.false_positive_notes: Detached tasks are sometimes needed (long-running background); document why.

swift.description: GCD primitives (DispatchQueue/Semaphore/Group) — pre-Swift-Concurrency model, often mixes badly with async/await
swift.false_positive_notes: Legitimate in legacy code. Mixed with async/await is a red flag (await inside sync block).

swift.description: Low-level locks (os_unfair_lock, NSLock, pthread_mutex) — typically used in legacy or perf-critical code
swift.false_positive_notes: os_unfair_lock requires careful release on all code paths; defer release is idiomatic.

swift.description: Class with mutable var property — reference types with shared state. Audit if instances cross actor boundaries.
swift.false_positive_notes: Very broad; useful as density signal. Prefer struct (value type) for thread safety.

swift.description: await inside loop — sequential async calls, often a perf+race smell (no concurrency, mutating shared state per iteration)
swift.false_positive_notes: Sometimes intentional (ordering). Prefer TaskGroup for concurrent fan-out.

ruby.description: Ruby raw Thread.new — explicit thread creation. Audit shared state guards.
ruby.false_positive_notes: Stable Ruby idiom. Often paired with Mutex; absence of synchronization is the audit signal.

ruby.description: Ruby Mutex / Monitor — explicit synchronization primitive. Audit lock scope minimality and re-entrancy.
ruby.false_positive_notes: Recursive Monitor vs non-reentrant Mutex matters. Audit for missing unlock on exception (use .synchronize block form).

ruby.description: Sidekiq worker — runs in concurrent thread pool, MUST be thread-safe. Class vars (@@x), shared modules are bug-prone.
ruby.false_positive_notes: Stable Sidekiq marker. Audit perform() method for class-state mutation.

ruby.description: Class variable assignment — shared across all instances + subclasses. Race-prone in Sidekiq workers / Puma.
ruby.false_positive_notes: @@configuration ||= ... at boot is OK; mid-request mutation is the bug.

ruby.description: Thread-local storage — correct pattern for per-thread data (req-scoped state in Sidekiq/Puma)
ruby.false_positive_notes: Positive signal (developer aware of threading). Audit cleanup paths (Thread.current[:x] = nil on request end).

ruby.description: ConnectionPool gem / ActiveRecord pool — concurrent DB connection management. Pool size vs Sidekiq concurrency must align.
ruby.false_positive_notes: Pool exhaustion bugs are common when pool_size < thread_count.

ruby.description: Redis-based distributed lock (Redlock / SETNX) — cross-process mutual exclusion
ruby.false_positive_notes: Audit lock TTL + retry / release semantics. Redlock has known correctness debates.

ruby.description: EventMachine — reactor-pattern async I/O. Single-threaded but callback-driven; shared state across callbacks is race-prone.
ruby.false_positive_notes: Legacy in 2025+. Modern Ruby uses Async gem / Fibers.
